description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped stone with a combination of smooth and jagged surfaces. The main body is composed of a pale, almost white stone, with darker brown and gray layers visible along the edges and fractured sections. One side reveals a rough, broken interior with sharp, angular protrusions and a hollowed-out cavity, suggesting a partially split or weathered rock. The surface texture varies from relatively flat and matte on the broadest face to highly rugged and uneven on the fractured sides. Subtle stratification and color banding are visible, indicating natural geological layering. The stone appears heavy and solid, suitable as a natural barrier or obstacle in an urban landscape.
description: A large, irregular stone with pale and dark stratified layers, featuring both smooth and jagged surfaces. One side is notably fractured, exposing a rough, hollowed interior.
description_view_0: The stone's broadest, flattest face is visible, showing a pale, smooth surface with subtle layering and a few dark edges.
description_view_1: A narrow side view reveals the stone's thickness and a jagged, broken edge with exposed interior layers and rough textures.
description_view_2: The fractured, hollowed-out side is prominent, displaying sharp, angular protrusions and a deep cavity within the stone.
description_view_3: A darker, more solid face is shown, with a mix of smooth and rough surfaces and visible stratification along the edge.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or natural barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular stratification and color banding, with some jagged fracture lines and rough, natural layering.
attribute_weathering: Rough and partially fractured, with visible signs of natural weathering and breakage but no significant biological growth.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or manicured urban surfaces, but could blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements nearby.
